bonuses.leadin: Ludwig praises Nathan for understanding the movement of knots in a cat’s cradle in this play. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Ludwig praises Nathan for understanding the movement of knots in a cat's cradle in this play. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this 2020 play which ends with Rosa responding “Auschwitz” to the names Sally, Mimi, Bella, Hermine, and Heini. Felicity Gill mimicked Gustav Klimt for a portrait featured in this play depicting Gretl, who dies of a brain tumor.
This playwright, who created the Riemann-obsessed mathematician Ludwig in Leopoldstadt, wrote Rosencrantz and Guildenstern are Dead.

Patrick Marber, who directed Leopoldstadt on Broadway, also directed a production of Travesties, where Henry Carr meets a founder of this movement. Tristan Tzara and Hugo Ball helped found this movement, whose members were affiliated with the Cabaret Voltaire.
parts_sanitized: Name this 2020 play which ends with Rosa responding "Auschwitz" to the names Sally, Mimi, Bella, Hermine, and Heini. Felicity Gill mimicked Gustav Klimt for a portrait featured in this play depicting Gretl, who dies of a brain tumor.
This playwright, who created the Riemann-obsessed mathematician Ludwig in Leopoldstadt, wrote Rosencrantz and Guildenstern are Dead.

Patrick Marber, who directed Leopoldstadt on Broadway, also directed a production of Travesties, where Henry Carr meets a founder of this movement. Tristan Tzara and Hugo Ball helped found this movement, whose members were affiliated with the Cabaret Voltaire.

bonuses.leadin: In a play by this author, Leo Rosenbaum offends Nathan and Rosa by having no recollection of his life as a Jew before changing his name to Leo Chamberlain. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: In a play by this author, Leo Rosenbaum offends Nathan and Rosa by having no recollection of his life as a Jew before changing his name to Leo Chamberlain. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this author who drew on the stories of his grandparents, who were Holocaust victims, for a 2020 play. This author used World War I as the backdrop of his play Travesties.
In this 2020 Stoppard play, the Nazis seize the Merz family's property during the Anschluss, after which it is revealed that Jacob is the legal son of Gretl and Fritz.
In Stoppard's play The Invention of Love, this poet reflects on his lifelong love for Moses Jackson while boarding a ferry to the afterlife. This poet wrote the collection A Shropshire Lad.
